acceptance_targets: [ch06-F001] PASS if the revised ending anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:365 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:383 contains one direct Nora-contact action (call/text/voicemail) with at least one quoted line and one immediate consequence (response, non-response, or logistical complication). FAIL if the chapter still ends only on internal planning/vow statements.

[ch06-F002] PASS if by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:120 the revised text includes an explicit employment-risk indicator that changes scene pressure (e.g., missed HR contact, deadline clock, or Eli actively dodging the inbox). FAIL if the first concrete job-risk signal still appears only at or after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:151.

[ch06-F003] PASS if in the revised exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:351, Imani has at least one probing follow-up question and one concrete domestic/material reference, while Theo keeps at least one direct boundary imperative; with speaker tags removed, lines remain clearly attributable. FAIL if both voices still read as the same command cadence.

[ch06-F004] PASS if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:73 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:75 contains no more than one sentence beginning with 'He had known' and at least one other realization rendered through concrete observed action/object detail rather than mirrored clause structure. FAIL if mirrored 'He had known/He had not known' repeats more than once.

[ch06-F005] PASS if the revised cited beats no longer all hinge on 'It was not ...' construction and at least two are rewritten as direct assertions/actions. FAIL if the same negation template remains the primary sentence shape in all cited locations.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised ending anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:365 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:383 contains one direct Nora-contact action (call/text/voicemail) with at least one quoted line and one immediate consequence (response, non-response, or logistical complication). FAIL if the chapter still ends only on internal planning/vow statements.
findings.problem: Sanitization/tension-defusal: after the termination reveal, the chapter postpones Nora-facing consequence and closes on self-instruction and vow language, replacing immediate dramatic consequence with summary and reassurance.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:233, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:365, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:383, replace the vow-based close with an on-page consequence beat (attempted call/text/voicemail to Nora, or failed attempt with concrete fallout). Keep Eli's anti-spin intent, but force it through present-moment action pressure rather than private resolution language.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if by snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:120 the revised text includes an explicit employment-risk indicator that changes scene pressure (e.g., missed HR contact, deadline clock, or Eli actively dodging the inbox). FAIL if the first concrete job-risk signal still appears only at or after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:151.
findings.problem: Chapter-level causal tension starts late: the domestic dinner scene runs long before the irreversible HR turn appears, so consequence momentum arrives after an extended low-threat plateau.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or repurpose early kitchen material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:91, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:135 so employment risk is braided in earlier (deadline pressure, avoidance behavior, or pending contact) before the inbox reveal at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:151 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:173.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if in the revised exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:351, Imani has at least one probing follow-up question and one concrete domestic/material reference, while Theo keeps at least one direct boundary imperative; with speaker tags removed, lines remain clearly attributable. FAIL if both voices still read as the same command cadence.
findings.problem: Idiolect separation softens under pressure: Theo and Imani converge into similar clipped managerial imperatives, and Imani's cadence turns slogan-like, reducing register distinctness required by the style bible.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework exchange anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:289, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:291, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:297, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:351 so Theo retains boundary/accountability terseness while Imani reclaims her own mixed cadence (concrete domestic framing plus probing follow-up question), avoiding aphoristic slogan beats that could belong to either speaker.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised span anchored at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:73 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:75 contains no more than one sentence beginning with 'He had known' and at least one other realization rendered through concrete observed action/object detail rather than mirrored clause structure. FAIL if mirrored 'He had known/He had not known' repeats more than once.
findings.problem: Recurring sentence construction tic: the repeated 'He had known ... He had not known ...' pattern is used multiple times in one beat and reads mechanical rather than discovered.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:73 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:75, keep one contrast statement and recast the remaining realizations with different syntax and a concrete action/image beat, so recognition unfolds instead of repeating a template.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if the revised cited beats no longer all hinge on 'It was not ...' construction and at least two are rewritten as direct assertions/actions. FAIL if the same negation template remains the primary sentence shape in all cited locations.
findings.problem: Negation-correction formula repeats ('It was not ...') across multiple moments, flattening cadence and signaling autopilot construction reuse.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the beats at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:55, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:101, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:103, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_06.md:111 by converting at least two negation-led lines into direct positive description or action, preserving meaning without repeating the same rhetorical scaffold.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into morally mixed behavior, bodily consequence, and economic detail when they are causally active. In edge cases, choose the more concrete and uncomfortable rendering unless it violates factual continuity; if two renderings are equally plausible, prefer the one that increases character accountability rather than reader reassurance.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene contains pain, sex, addiction, debt panic, or humiliation, abstract phrasing is treated as a failed draft and must be rewritten with embodied specificity.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the spoken default; uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, legal precision, or deliberate emotional distance.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter architectures across the book: confrontation chapters, document-information turns, labor-process chapters, transit interiority chapters, and quiet relational chapters. Consecutive chapters must differ in scene density or dialogue-to-interiority ratio.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary American literary realism: precise, unsentimental, image-aware, and concrete under stress.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third-person anchored to Eli, with no unmarked POV drift.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Braided cadence: reflective medium-length sentences interrupted by blunt short lines at impact moments.
